task_completion.description: Load the Landsat multi-spectral TIFF image into SNAP Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Product Explorer panel in SNAP Desktop shows 'landsat_multispectral' (or similar) listed as an imported product.

task_completion.description: Import the reserve boundary shapefile into the loaded product.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'reserve_boundary' vector is visible under the Vector Data node of the loaded product in the Product Explorer, or a diamond-shaped vector boundary is overlaid on the image view.

task_completion.description: Open and configure a masking tool using the imported vector.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A masking dialog (e.g., Raster > Masks > Land/Sea Mask) is open, and the vector 'reserve_boundary' is explicitly selected or checked as the masking geometry/mask.

task_completion.description: Generate the masked product where the exterior is set to NoData or 0.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A new product is created in the Product Explorer, and its image view displays a diamond-shaped area of valid pixels with the surrounding exterior completely masked out (appearing as solid black, white, or transparent).

task_completion.description: Export the masked product in ENVI format to the target directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export Product' dialog is visible, specifically set to ENVI format with the destination path '/home/ga/snap_exports/', or a file explorer/terminal shows the exported ENVI files (e.g., .hdr and .img/.data) in that directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the ESA SNAP Desktop GUI to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the SNAP Desktop graphical interface rather than relying solely on terminal commands (like 'gpt') or Python scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The masking was performed using SNAP's spatial/vector tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: The masked diamond shape is a result of SNAP's processing tools (evidenced by tool dialogs and progress bars) and not manually drawn or edited using a basic image painting tool.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely exported to ENVI format.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used SNAP's File > Export > ENVI menu (or similar export function) rather than just renaming a .tif file extension to .hdr or .envi in the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The shapefile forms a diamond shape. When applied as a mask, the resulting image should show a valid diamond-shaped area covering exactly 32% of the full image (or 50% if the tool cropped it to the bounding box). The exterior must be completely masked out. The final export must be in ENVI format (which typically produces a .hdr header file) and saved exactly to '/home/ga/snap_exports/'.
